today_news.title: ChatGPT's new health upgrade beats doctor-written answers, OpenAI says
today_news.summary: OpenAI has upgraded ChatGPT's healthcare capabilities with GPT-5.5 Instant. In the company's own comparative tests, the model now outscores answers written by doctors in accuracy, clarity, and completeness. The error rate for health-related statements has dropped by 71 percent, according to OpenAI.

today_news.title: Website "In the Weights" shows whether AI models know who you are
today_news.summary: Two former OpenAI employees have built a website called "In the Weights" that reveals which people AI models can recall purely from their training data. A strength score of up to 996 shows how deeply a person is embedded, with Mozart, Shakespeare, and Taylor Swift topping the list.

today_news.title: The inevitable weakness of metrics
today_news.summary: There are plenty of useful things a metric can reveal. There are even more it can obscure or corrupt. today_news.title: Anthropic brings Artifacts to Claude Code, letting teams share live pages from coding sessions
today_news.summary: Claude Code can now turn work results into interactive web pages called "artifacts" and share them with your team. The pages pull from the full session context, update automatically when something changes, and keep a version history.

today_news.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
today_news.summary: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. today_news.title: Geoengineering still faces major practical challenges
today_news.summary: Solar geoengineering is often portrayed as a sort of emergency brake. today_news.title: Alleged China ties at SK Telecom alarmed US officials and triggered Anthropic crisis
today_news.summary: SK Telecom had access to Anthropic's AI model Claude Mythos through the company's partner program Project Glasswing until the White House stepped in. US officials saw ties between the South Korean conglomerate and China.
